content: read reviews/856.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/856.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/856.md.

    52→- [x] Parameters `(k : ℕ) (hk : k ≥ 3)` are theorem-level parameters, which is fine since no `answer()` is used (the quantifier rule only applies to `answer()`-bearing theorems).

file.content:
# Review: `deepmind/856.lean` — Erdős Problem 856

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports` (line 17).
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line (line 19).
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 856`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/856`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (defines $f_k(N)$ and asks to estimate it).
- [x] Bibliography entries present: `[Er70]` (Erdős 1970), `[TaZh25b]` (Tang–Zhang 2025).

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos856 ... end Erdos856`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos856`).
- [x] `open Finset Real` appears **before** the namespace block (line 35).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `NoPairwiseLcmClique` (line 42) has a LaTeX docstring (lines 39–41) describing the predicate.
- [x] `fk` (line 48) has a LaTeX docstring (lines 46–48) describing the supremum.
- [x] `fk` is marked `noncomputable` (uses `sSup`).
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] `NoPairwiseLcmClique` uses `UpperCamelCase` (it is a `Prop`).
- [x] `fk` uses `lowerCamelCase` (function returning `ℝ`).
- [ ] No `local notation` used — N/A, none needed for this problem.
- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks on definitions — optional but could help verify `NoPairwiseLcmClique` and `fk` behave as expected.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] All three declarations use the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_856` (line 60), matching the namespace number in `snake_case`.
- [x] All theorem names follow `snake_case` convention.
- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line** (e.g., `@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]`).
- [x] All three theorems have docstrings.
- [x] Docstrings reference source papers: `[Er70]` for the main theorem, `[TaZh25b]` for both variants.

### `answer()` usage

The original problem says "Estimate $f_k(N)$." This is a directive to establish bounds — **Case 3** (direct assertion). The file correctly states bound results directly without an `answer()` wrapper.

- [x] Case 3 applied correctly: theorems state bounds as direct propositions (no `answer()` needed).
- [x] Parameters `(k : ℕ) (hk : k ≥ 3)` are theorem-level parameters, which is fine since no `answer()` is used (the quantifier rule only applies to `answer()`-bearing theorems).

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Every theorem has exactly one `@[category ...]`.
- [x] All three theorems use `research solved` — correct, as the bounds have been established by Erdős [Er70] and Tang–Zhang [TaZh25b] but are not formally verified.
- [x] `research solved` theorems contain `sorry` in proof bodies — consistent (solved but not yet formally verified).

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Every theorem has exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (two: `5` and `11`).
- [x] Numbers in ascending numeric order: `5 11`. ✓
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] Both are valid AMS MSC2020 codes: `5` (combinatorics), `11` (number theory). Appropriate for a problem about LCM-free subsets and harmonic sums.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] All three theorems use the multi-line `by\n  sorry` format (lines 63–64, 78–79, 96–97). Acceptable.
- [x] No `sorry` in `def` or `abbrev` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches included.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- [x] Variants named `erdos_856.variants.tang_zhang_lower` (line 74) and `erdos_856.variants.tang_zhang_upper` (line 92).
- [x] All variants live in the same file as the main theorem.
- [x] Each variant has its own `@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]` attributes.
- [x] Both variants have descriptive docstrings explaining the Tang–Zhang bounds and their relationship to the sunflower conjecture.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `∀`, `∃`, `∈`, `⊆`, `≥`, `≤`, `∧`, `¬`, `≠`, `ℕ`, `ℝ`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (<100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not run as part of this review. Structural analysis shows no issues; the file should compile with expected `sorry` warnings.
- [ ] Custom linter compliance not machine-verified, but manual inspection shows conformance with all five linter rules (`copyright`, `category_attribute`, `ams_attribute`, `answer_attribute`, `namespace`).

---

## Summary

**Status: PASS**

The file is well-structured and conforms to all checklist requirements. The formalization cleanly separates the original Erdős upper bound [Er70] from the more recent Tang–Zhang refinements [TaZh25b]. The `NoPairwiseLcmClique` predicate and `fk` supremum definition are clearly documented and correctly typed. All three theorems use appropriate `research solved` categorization with correct AMS codes. No issues found.

content.text: The review status is **PASS**. No changes needed.
